case_title: MALOUK GHAYOUR FARAJI et al VS. ANCHORAGE ENTERPRISES, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIA et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT ANCHORAGE ENTERPRISES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST, SECOND, FOURTH, SIXTH AND SEVEN CAUSES OF ACTION: 1) 1ST & 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION: DEMURRER IS OVERRULED WITH THE UNDERSTANDING THAT THE CLAIMS ARE LIMITED TO THOSE STATED IN THE WRITTEN CONTRACT. IF EITHER OF THE TYPES OF MISREPRESENTATION ARE OTHER THAN THOSE STATED IN THE CONTRACT, THEN THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO SPECIFICALLY ALLEGE WHO MADE WHAT MISREPRESENTATIONS. 2) 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO SPECIFY WHAT COVENANTS ARE BREACHED THAT ARE NOT COVERED BY BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. 3) 6TH & 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO SPECIFY THE NATURE OF THE PARTIES RELATIONSHIP AND WHAT ROLE PLAINTIFFS' AGE PLAYS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR#:1558
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING STIPULATED JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT DEFENDANT ANCHORAGE ENTERPRISES, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIA BILITY COMPANY RECOVER FROM PLAINTIFF FARAJI, MALOUK GHAYOUR INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA CAFE ESPRESSO FARAJI, MINA GHAYOUR INDIVIDUALY AND DBA CAFE ESPRESSO $85,000.00 PRINCIPAL, TOTAL JUDGMENT $85,000.00, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED EXECUTION OF JUDGMENT BE STAYED UNTIL 6/27/05, EXCEPT THAT ANCHORAGE ENTERPRISES MAY RECORD AND ABSTRACT AGAINST RESIDENCE LOCATED AT 17 GREENWOOD BAY DRIVE, TIBURON, CA. 94920. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED THAT ANCHORAGE BE ENTITLED TO REASONABLE AND NECESSARY COSTS, INCLUDING ATTORNEYS' FEES INCURRED IN ENFORCEMENT OF JUDGMENT. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT (NO SASE - 206 OUTBOX)

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly for trial by jury.

Appearances:
John Gary Warner, Esq. on behalf of Malouk Faraji, Mina Foraji and Fooad Laghaer
Ivan Gold, Esq. on behalf of Anchorage Enterprises, LLC.

This matter settled after being sent to Judge Pro Tem. This is a global settlement of case #427951 consol. w/#608868. Terms of the settlement were as follows: Case number 427951 was dismissed. As to case number #608868 - Malouk Faraji, Mina Foraji and Fooad Laghaer stipulated to judgment in the sum of $85,000.00. An abstract of judgment will be attached as a lien on the Tiburon home. The parties agreed to stay enforcement of the judgment for 90 days commencing today 2/28/05 date. Parties agreed to CC 1542 waiver. Each side to bear own costs. The Court to retain jurisdiction pursuant to CCP 664.6. All parties agreed to the terms of the settlement on the record. Defense counsel to prepare judgment. Hon. David L. Ballati; Clerks: V. Mu/D. Notice Of Hearing Of Demurrer To The First, Second, Fourth, Sixth And Seven Causes Of Action In Pltf'S Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-24-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael J. Betz, Esq., Allen Matkins (415) 273-7453, for Defendant and Moving Party Anchorage Enterprises, LLC
RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING:
DEFENDANT ANCHORAGE ENTERPRISES, LLC'S Demurrer To The First, Second, Fourth, Sixth And Seven Causes Of Action:
1) 1st & 2nd Causes of Action: Demurrer is OVERRULED with the understanding that the claims are limited to those stated in the written contract. If either of the types of misrepresentation are other than those stated in the contract, then the Demurrer is SUSTAINED with 10 days leave to amend to specifically allege who made what misrepresentations.
2) 4th Cause of Action: Demurrer is SUSTAINED with 10 days leave to amend to specify what covenants are breached that are not covered by Breach of Contract cause of action.
3) 6th & 7th Cause of Action: Demurrer is SUSTAINED with 10 days leave to amend to specify the nature of the parties relationship and what role Plaintiffs' age plays. Order signed. (JLW/301/DS) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
